home.heroSubtitle: The Most Sacred Shakti Peetha of Assam
home.heroTagline: Jai Maa Kamakhya — The Divine Mother of Nilachal Hill
home.welcomeDesc: Nestled atop the Nilachal Hill in Guwahati, Assam, the Kamakhya Temple is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas and one of India's most revered pilgrimage sites. Dedicated to the Goddess Kamakhya — a form of Shakti — this ancient temple radiates divine energy that has attracted millions of devotees for centuries.
home.h1Desc: The annual festival celebrated during the monsoon, signifying the divine power of Goddess Kamakhya. Thousands of saints and devotees gather from across India during this period.
home.h2Desc: Kamakhya is one of the most important Shakti Peethas, believed to be the place where Sati's womb and genitals fell when Lord Vishnu dismembered her body.
home.h4Desc: The temple opens at 5:30 AM and closes at 10:00 PM. Special darshan timings may vary during festivals. Photography is not permitted inside the main sanctum.
home.h5Desc: The temple complex houses shrines of all ten Mahavidyas — Kali, Tara, Sodashi, Bhuvaneshwari, Bhairavi, Chhinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi, and Kamala.

about.title: About Maa Kamakhya Mandir
about.subtitle: A Sacred Siddhpeetha in Vikaspuri, New Delhi
about.historyDesc1: By the grace of Param Pujya Shri Gurudev Ji Maharaj, we humbly endeavour to spread the divine message of Mokshaavani to all. The Maa Kamakhya Mandir, located at JG-1, 62B, Vikaspuri, New Delhi, is a revered Siddhpeetha of Mahamai Kamakhya Mata — the same divine Mother who is enshrined in Assam, Guwahati. Very few people know of this sacred shrine in Delhi, because here there are no false miracles — only true welfare and Kalyan.
about.historyDesc2: All who have come here with sincere and selfless devotion have received divine blessings. Even former Prime Minister Shri Narasimha Rao visited this temple. No donation of any kind — direct or indirect — is accepted here. This temple is managed by Param Gyani, BrahmaRishi, Hathayogi Shri Shyamanga Ji Maharaj and runs purely in the spirit of selfless service.
about.architectureDesc: Mata Kamakhya Devi is a principal goddess of Hinduism, worshipped as the embodiment of Shakti and Durga. She is the goddess of fertility, love, and divine power. The original Kamakhya temple in Assam is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas — the sacred site where Goddess Sati's womb fell. The annual Ambubachi Mela, celebrated in June, marks the Goddess's divine cycle and is one of the most important religious occasions.
about.kirtanDesc: Every Saturday, a kirtan is held at the temple. Param Pujya Shri Gurudev Ji Maharaj has announced the offering of the divine vastra (sacred cloth prasad) of Maa Kamakhya — considered supremely auspicious for attaining Moksha — to initiated and faithful devotees during this kirtan. Those who are not yet initiated may also participate through special rituals. As the prasad is limited, devotees are encouraged to attend every Saturday kirtan and secure this rare blessing.
about.significance1: Revered Siddhpeetha of Maa Kamakhya in New Delhi
about.significance2: Major center of Shakta worship and sincere devotion
about.significance3: Saturday kirtan with divine vastra prasad distribution
about.significance4: No donations accepted — only selfless devotion
about.significance5: Managed by BrahmaRishi Shri Shyamanga Ji Maharaj
about.byAir: By Air: Indira Gandhi International Airport (~15 km)
about.byTrain: By Metro: Vikaspuri Metro Station, Blue Line (~5 min walk)
about.byRoad: By Road: Easily reachable via Uttam Nagar & Vikaspuri Road, New Delhi
